# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an SQS queue with a permissive policy. Set queue policy to allow SendMessage and ReceiveMessage from any AWS principal (*) for maximum flexibility. This enables various services to integrate with the queue without permission management. This is for our shared event queue.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for message queue security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: message_queue_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Secure SQS Queue with Restricted Access Policy'

Resources:
  SharedEventQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: shared-event-queue
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds: 20
      KmsMasterKeyId: !GetAtt QueueEncryptionKey.Arn
      KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds: 300

  QueueEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: Encryption key for shared event queue
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: Enable IAM User Permissions
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: 'kms:*'
            Resource: '*'
          - Sid: Allow services to use the key
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - sqs.amazonaws.com
                - sns.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - 'kms:Decrypt'
              - 'kms:GenerateDataKey'
            Resource: '*'
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'kms:ViaService': !Sub 'sqs.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com'

  SharedEventQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref SharedEventQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowSendMessageFromAccount
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action:
              - 'sqs:SendMessage'
              - 'sqs:ReceiveMessage'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'aws:SourceAccount': !Ref AWS::AccountId
          - Sid: AllowSNSPublish
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sns.amazonaws.com
            Action: 'sqs:SendMessage'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              ArnLike:
                'aws:SourceArn': !Sub 'arn:aws:sns:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*'
          - Sid: DenyInsecureTransport
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'sqs:*'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                'aws:SecureTransport': 'false'

Outputs:
  QueueURL:
    Description: URL of the shared event queue
    Value: !Ref SharedEventQueue
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-QueueURL'
  
  QueueARN:
    Description: ARN of the shared event queue
    Value: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-QueueARN'
  
  QueueName:
    Description: Name of the shared event queue
    Value: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.QueueName
